title: Ethical Transition, Rejection Learning, and Continuing Career Development · Hiring Decision Lens: First Ninety Days
summary: After an outcome, the candidate either stops learning, extracts certainty from sparse feedback, burns relationships, or carries confidential material into the next role. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the first ninety days increment in first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.
Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict First Ninety Days · Theory First Ninety Days
steps.instruction:
theory case — A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the First Ninety Days contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict First Ninety Days · Theory First Ninety Days.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments. For Predict First Ninety Days · Theory First Ninety Days, keep First Ninety Days connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which First Ninety Days condition decided your prediction.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The worked example variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments. A defensible solution applies First Ninety Days, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A new hire should prove value by making major changes immediately. This fails the First Ninety Days contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook for Software Development and Quality Assurance (2024 data and 2024-2034 projections; page modified 2025-08-28) — Controls U.S.-specific occupation duties, collaboration expectations, important qualities, education norms, wage interpretation, and projection caveats.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.
